topic: Kilo Code Review 2026: Cline Fork with Orchestrator Mode and Inline Autocomplete

key_points: Covers 500+ models, open source, free tier with $20 signup credits
Focuses on multi-mode workflow: Architect, Code, Ask, Debug
Emphasizes zero-markup pricing as main differentiator vs Cursor/Copilot
Head-to-head comparison: Orchestrator Mode vs Cline's single-agent flow
Covers Memory Bank, JetBrains support, Slack bot as Kilo-only features
Concludes: pick Cline for simplicity, Kilo Code for multi-model orchestration
Detailed review of zero-markup pricing model and Kilo Pass ($19/mo)
Notes deprecation of Orchestrator mode in favor of built-in subagent delegation
Highlights 1.5M+ users and #1 ranking on OpenRouter
Hands-on tested review covering VS Code and JetBrains installation
Notes that fast feature shipping sometimes leads to rough edges
Deep dive into open-source credentials and Apache-2.0 license
Examines the Cline → Roo Code → Kilo Code fork lineage
Evaluates multi-agent coordination and parallel task routing

stats.fact: Kilo Code has 19,200+ GitHub stars as of May 2026
Kilo Code has 1.5 million+ users and is #1 on OpenRouter
Kilo Code has processed 25 trillion tokens
Kilo Code raised $8M in seed funding in December 2025 led by Cota Capital
Kilo Code supports 500+ AI models from 60+ providers
Kilo Code has 3,636 forks on GitHub
Kilo Code offers $20 in free credits on signup with no markup on provider API costs
Cline (original) has 58K+ GitHub stars and 5M+ VS Code installs for comparison
Roo Code (Kilo's parent fork) has 22K+ stars and 1.2M installs
Kilo Pass subscription starts at $19/month for bundled credits

angles: Fork-of-a-fork story: Cline → Roo Code → Kilo Code, and why that matters for trust and maintenance
The VC-backed open-source paradox: $8M funding raises sustainability questions for a free, MIT-licensed tool
Zero-markup pricing as a business model disruption vs Cursor Pro ($20/mo) and GitHub Copilot Pro ($10/mo)
Orchestrator mode evolution: deprecated in favor of native subagent delegation — a feature removed is a signal of maturity
Memory Bank → AGENTS.md migration: how Kilo Code is aligning with the broader ecosystem standard
Cross-platform play: VS Code + JetBrains + CLI + Slack bot makes Kilo the most platform-diverse open-source agent
500+ models at provider cost vs locked-in model tiers — the model-agnostic bet for enterprise developers
Who should NOT use Kilo Code: users needing stability over features should consider Cline or Roo Code
keywords: best open source ai coding agent 2026
